name: Tightrope
flavor: The precision discipline: a performer who has spent so long balanced on a single strand of wire high above the crowd that solid ground feels generous, and every strike lands exactly where it was always going to.

features.name: Perfect Balance
features.description: You ignore the effects of narrow, unstable, or slippery surfaces: you can move across a tightrope, ledge, ice, rubble, or similar footing at your full speed, without needing an ability check, and you don't fall from such a surface. Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws and ability checks made to avoid being knocked prone or moved against your will.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20. Whenever you score a critical hit, you regain 1 Momentum (no more than once per turn, and never above your current cap).

features.name: Wire-Walker's Riposte
features.description: You have spent years making attackers commit to a strike that finds only wire and empty air. When a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to answer from perfect balance: make one melee weapon attack against it. Like all your weapon attacks (see Improved Critical), this scores a critical hit on a 19 or 20. Because your own footing is never in question, on a hit you throw the target off its line — it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the start of your next turn. This is distinct from your base Riposte, which triggers on a clean Full Dodge and spends Momentum at no reaction cost; Wire-Walker's Riposte instead punishes any miss and costs your reaction. It uses the same single reaction as Parry, so landing a Parry and a Riposte in the same round relies on Flowing Reflexes (4th level) returning your reaction after a successful Parry.
